metadata.title: ICH S-Series Nonclinical Study Requirements
metadata.description: Comprehensive extraction of all nonclinical study requirements from ICH S1-S13 guidelines

S1A.title: Need for Carcinogenicity Studies of Pharmaceuticals
S1A.scope: All pharmaceuticals; determines when carcinogenicity studies are required

requirements.trigger: Continuous clinical use >= 6 months, or frequent intermittent use for chronic/recurrent conditions
requirements.species: Rodent (rat default, mouse for second study)
requirements.timing: Completed before marketing approval application; NOT needed before large-scale clinical trials unless special concern
special_conditions: Life-threatening diseases with no alternative: can be post-approval
Drugs used 3 months also expected to be used 6 months

requirements.trigger: Any of: carcinogenic potential in product class, SAR suggesting risk, preneoplastic lesions in repeat-dose tox, long-term tissue retention
special_conditions: Required regardless of clinical duration

requirements.trigger: Unequivocally genotoxic compounds intended for chronic use
special_conditions: Long-term carcinogenicity studies NOT needed for trans-species carcinogens

requirements.trigger: Concern for photocarcinogenic potential with dermal application

exemptions: Infrequent or short-duration use (anaesthetics, radiolabeled imaging agents)
Oncolytic agents for advanced systemic disease
Endogenous peptides/proteins at physiological replacement levels (unless cause for concern)
Ocular route (unless cause for concern or significant systemic exposure)

S1B_and_S1BR1.title: Testing for Carcinogenicity of Pharmaceuticals (Original + R1 Addendum)
S1B_and_S1BR1.scope: All pharmaceuticals requiring carcinogenicity testing per S1A (excludes biotech per S6(R1))

requirements.species: Rat (default; in absence of clear evidence favoring another species)
special_conditions: Species selection based on pharmacology, repeat-dose tox, metabolism, TK, route

requirements.study_type: Additional in vivo carcinogenicity study (short/medium-term transgenic model)
requirements.dose_groups: 3 dose levels + control; high dose at 50-fold plasma AUC ratio (rodent:human)
special_conditions: Prioritized per 3R principles over 2-year mouse study
Wild-type rasH2-Wt littermates for dose range-finding

requirements.study_type: Second long-term carcinogenicity study (alternative to transgenic model)
requirements.species: Mouse (typically, if rat used for first study)
special_conditions: Alternative to S1B-02

endpoints: Histopathology from repeat-dose tox (emphasis on 6-month rat)
requirements.timing: Before deciding on 2-year rat study; regulatory consultation recommended
special_conditions: Three outcomes: LIKELY carcinogenic (no rat study), UNLIKELY (no rat study, mouse still recommended), UNCERTAIN (standard S1B approach)
Mouse study remains recommended even when WoE waives rat study
woe_factors_favoring_waiver: Well-characterized target biology not associated with cancer pathways
No off-target concerns from secondary pharmacology
No hyperplastic/atypical cellular changes in chronic tox
No endocrine perturbation or adequately explained

prerequisite_data: Pharmacodynamics in animals and humans (per S1C)
Repeat-dose toxicology in any species including nonrodents
Immunosuppressant, hormonal, or other risk-factor activity data

S1CR2.title: Dose Selection for Carcinogenicity Studies
S1CR2.scope: Dose selection criteria for all carcinogenicity studies

high_dose_criteria.definition: Dose predicted to produce minimum toxic effect over carcinogenicity study; no more than 10% body weight gain decrease vs controls
Dose producing rodent plasma AUC 25x human AUC at MRHD; applies to ALL pharmaceuticals including those with positive genotox signals
PD response precluding further dose escalation (e.g., hypotension, clotting inhibition)
1500 mg/kg/day when human dose <= 500 mg/day and rodent exposure >= 10x human

S2R1.title: Guidance on Genotoxicity Testing and Data Interpretation
S2R1.scope: New small molecule drug substances only; excludes biologics

requirements.study_type: Standard Test Battery Option 1 (2 in vitro + 1 in vivo)

tests.species: S. typhimurium TA98, TA100, TA1535, plus TA1537/TA97/TA97a, plus TA102 or E. coli WP2 uvrA or WP2 uvrA(pKM101)
endpoints: Revertant colony counts (base substitution + frameshift mutations)
tests.conditions: With and without S9 metabolic activation; max 5000 microg/plate

tests.test: In vitro mammalian cell assay (one of three)
options: Mouse lymphoma Tk gene mutation assay (MLA)
tests.species: Mammalian cell lines (CHO, CHL, TK6, L5178Y)
endpoints: Chromosomal aberrations / micronuclei / Tk mutant frequency + colony sizing
tests.conditions: Top concentration: 1 mM or 0.5 mg/ml (whichever lower); cytotoxicity limit ~50% reduction; with and without S9

tests.species: Rats or mice (bone marrow or peripheral blood)
endpoints: Ratio of immature to total erythrocytes
tests.dose_levels: 3 dose levels; limit dose 2000 mg/kg (acute) or 1000 mg/kg (14+ days)

requirements.timing: Complete standard battery before initial clinical trials (Phase I)

requirements.study_type: Standard Test Battery Option 2 (1 in vitro + 2 in vivo tissues)

tests.test: In vivo assessment with two tissues
tests.tissue_1: Micronuclei in hematopoietic cells (bone marrow or blood)
tests.tissue_2: DNA strand breakage in liver (Comet assay preferred)
tests.conditions: Preferred when short-lived reactive metabolites generated in liver

requirements.study_type: In vivo Comet assay (follow-up / Option 2 component)
endpoints: DNA strand breaks via single cell gel electrophoresis
requirements.sampling_times: Single dose: few hours + 24 hours post-treatment; multiple dose: 2-6 hours after last dose
requirements.timing: As part of battery or follow-up

requirements.study_type: In vivo transgenic rodent mutation assay (follow-up)
requirements.species: Transgenic mice (Big Blue, MutaMouse, gpt delta)
endpoints: Mutation frequency in transgenes (lacZ, lacI, cII, gpt)
requirements.timing: Follow-up for positive findings or target organ assessment

exposure_demonstration.required_when: In vitro test positive or not done and in vivo test negative
evidence_options: Cytotoxicity measures (change in PCE ratio, reduced mitotic index, liver toxicity by histopath/biochem)
Drug measurement in blood/plasma or target tissue

follow_up_strategies.positive_in_vitro_ames_negative: Weight-of-evidence assessment; may need additional in vitro mechanistic studies OR two in vivo assays in appropriate tissues
follow_up_strategies.positive_ames: Extensive in vivo follow-up warranted; risk-benefit analysis for patients
follow_up_strategies.positive_in_vivo_MN: Evaluate non-genotoxic factors (disturbed erythropoiesis); centromere staining to distinguish breakage vs loss
follow_up_strategies.positive_result_S9_dependent: Verify metabolic activation responsible; follow-up in vivo in liver

S3A.title: Toxicokinetics: Assessment of Systemic Exposure in Toxicity Studies
S3A.scope: TK measurements integrated into all nonclinical toxicity studies

requirements.species: All species used in toxicity studies
requirements.matrix: Plasma, serum, or whole blood (primary); urine/tissue where appropriate

sampling_approaches.monitor: 1-3 samples per interval (estimate C(time) or Cmax)
sampling_approaches.profile: 4-8 samples per interval (estimate Cmax, C(time), AUC)

requirements.timing: Throughout nonclinical program; first repeat-dose TK normally 14+ days

requirements.approach: Profiling or monitoring at start AND end of treatment period

dose_levels.low_dose_NOAEL: Exposure should equal or just exceed maximum expected clinical exposure
dose_levels.intermediate: Appropriate multiple/fraction of low or high

requirements.timing: Before and concurrent with FIH studies

requirements.approach: Monitoring on a few occasions; not considered essential beyond 6 months
requirements.purpose: Confirm exposure consistent with dose-ranging study profiles

requirements.approach: PK information preferable before initiating repro studies; consider pregnant vs non-pregnant kinetics

microsampling.benefit: Can eliminate TK satellite groups; allows direct correlation of safety and exposure data
microsampling.applicable_studies: Any toxicology study (single-dose, repeat-dose, carcinogenicity, juvenile, reproductive)
microsampling.validation: Method must assess LLOQ, accuracy, precision, matrix effects, stability; comparability assessment between methods may be needed

S3B.title: Pharmacokinetics: Guidance for Repeated Dose Tissue Distribution Studies
S3B.scope: Tissue distribution studies; determines when repeated dose studies are needed

requirements.requirement_level: Standard requirement as part of nonclinical program
requirements.purpose: Distribution and accumulation information relative to potential sites of action

requirements.requirement_level: NOT routinely required; only when triggered
triggers: Tissue half-life significantly exceeds plasma half-life AND > 2x dosing interval
Steady-state levels markedly higher than predicted from single-dose kinetics
Unexpected histopathological changes not predicted from other studies
requirements.method: Radiolabeled compounds or alternatives with sufficient sensitivity
requirements.duration: Minimum 1 week; extended if steady state not reached; normally not beyond 3 weeks
endpoints: Unchanged compound AND metabolites in organs/tissues with extensive accumulation

S4.title: Duration of Chronic Toxicity Testing in Animals
S4.scope: All medicinal products except biotechnology-derived (covered by S6)

special_conditions: 12-month studies usually not necessary
12-month studies usually not necessary; EU accepts 6-month non-rodent

S5R3.title: Detection of Reproductive and Developmental Toxicity
S5R3.scope: All pharmaceuticals, biopharmaceuticals, vaccines; excludes cellular/gene therapies, tissue-engineered products

requirements.species: Rodent (rat preferred); biopharmaceuticals may use rodent/rabbit if pharmacologically active; NHP histopath of repro tissues from 3-month repeat-dose tox can substitute

administration_period.males: 2+ weeks prior to cohabitation through confirmation of mating
administration_period.females: 2+ weeks prior to cohabitation through implantation (GD6)

endpoints: Estrous cycle evaluation (daily, 2 weeks before cohabitation)
Cesarean section mid-gestation (GD13-15): corpora lutea, implantation sites, live/dead embryos
requirements.timing: Before Phase 3 (large-scale trials including WOCBP); male fertility can be deferred to pre-NDA if no repro organ toxicity in repeat-dose
special_conditions: If testicular toxicity in repeat-dose: extend male pre-cohabitation to 10 weeks (full spermatogenic cycle)
Both sexes treated together if no fertility effects anticipated

species.biopharmaceuticals: One or two species if pharmacologically relevant; NHP ePPND can substitute
species.vaccines: Single species (rabbits, rats, or mice) sufficient

requirements.group_size: At least 16 pregnant females per group
requirements.timing: Two-species EFD data needed before WOCBP in large Phase 3 trials

requirements.GLP: Optional (required if used to defer definitive EFD)
requirements.purpose: Can support limited inclusion of up to 150 WOCBP for up to 3 months before definitive EFD

f1_preweaning_endpoints: Physical development (eye opening, pinna unfolding, surface righting, auditory startle, air righting)
f1_postweaning_endpoints: Learning and memory (complex learning, locomotor activity, startle with prepulse inhibition)

requirements.study_type: Enhanced Pre- and Postnatal Development (ePPND) -- NHP only
f1_endpoints: Neurobehavioural test battery (at least 1 interval in first 2 weeks)
requirements.timing: Can substitute for separate EFD + PPND in NHP; report at marketing application

dose_selection.biopharmaceuticals: Maximum pharmacological effect OR ~10-fold exposure multiple (whichever higher)
dose_selection.low_dose: 1-5 fold multiple of human exposure at MRHD

risk_assessment_thresholds.increased_concern: NOAEL at exposures < 10-fold human exposure at MRHD
risk_assessment_thresholds.known_teratogens_NOAEL: < 4-fold exposure at MRHD in at least one species

S6R1.title: Preclinical Safety Evaluation of Biotechnology-Derived Pharmaceuticals
S6R1.scope: Biotechnology-derived pharmaceuticals (proteins, peptides, mAbs, ADCs); only in pharmacologically relevant species

special_conditions: Two relevant species for short-term (up to 1 month); one sufficient for longer if comparable tox
Studies in non-relevant species are misleading and discouraged
When no relevant species: use transgenic animals expressing human receptor

requirements.species: Relevant species only; two for short-term, one for long-term if comparable

duration.short_term_use_up_to_7_days: 2 weeks adequate for clinical studies and marketing
duration.chronic_indications: 6 months maximum (longer has not provided useful info)

requirements.high_dose: Higher of: max intended pharmacological effect OR ~10-fold exposure multiple over max clinical exposure
requirements.recovery_period: Generally included; complete recovery not essential

special_conditions: ADA in animals NOT predictive of human immunogenicity
ADA alone should NOT be sole criterion for early study termination

requirements.method: Reproductive tract evaluation in 3+ month repeat-dose tox using sexually mature NHPs
special_conditions: Mating studies not practical for NHP

requirements.timing: During Phase III with report at marketing; or interim report before Phase III if pregnancy cannot be prevented

requirements.requirement: NOT applicable to biotechnology-derived pharmaceuticals; NOT needed
requirements.exception: Cause for concern (e.g., organic linker in conjugated product / ADC)

requirements.approach: Weight-of-evidence preferred; standard 2-year rodent bioassays generally inappropriate
special_conditions: If WoE does not suggest risk: no additional testing; single rodent study may be considered if biologically active and non-immunogenic in rodents

requirements.approach: Can be incorporated into tox/PD studies for highly specific receptor-targeting products; more extensive evaluation for novel class

S7A.title: Safety Pharmacology Studies for Human Pharmaceuticals
S7A.scope: New chemical entities; biotechnology products if warranted

requirements.species: Conscious or anesthetized animals (typically dog or NHP; may include rodent)

special_conditions: Clinical observation alone NOT adequate; quantitative methods required

requirements.trigger: Concerns from core battery, tox studies, clinical trials, or literature
requirements.timing: During clinical development or before approval

requirements.trigger: Cause for concern or pharmacological properties warrant

exemptions: Locally applied agents with well-characterized pharmacology and low systemic exposure
Cytotoxic agents for end-stage cancer (per S9)
Biotech products with highly specific receptor targeting (endpoints in tox/PD studies suffice)
New salt form with similar PK/PD

S7B.title: Nonclinical Evaluation of Delayed Ventricular Repolarization (QT Prolongation)
S7B.scope: New chemical entities; biotechnology products when appropriate

requirements.test_system: hERG-transfected cell lines or native cardiac myocytes (NOT rat/mouse)
special_conditions: Positive control required in every study
Concentrations spanning and exceeding max therapeutic plasma concentration
Competition binding NOT a substitute for voltage clamp
Adult rat/mouse NOT appropriate (Ito-dominant repolarization)

requirements.species: Dog, monkey, swine, rabbit, ferret, guinea pig (NOT rat/mouse)
requirements.duration: Generally single dose; can combine with S7A CV core battery
special_conditions: Telemetry in unrestrained conscious animals preferred
TK monitoring recommended
HR correction formula choice must be justified

requirements.trigger: Inconsistencies among nonclinical studies or with clinical findings
endpoints: Action potential parameters in isolated cardiac preparations

requirements.description: Scientific evaluation integrating all S7B results with PD, PK, tox, clinical data
requirements.timing: Before FIH and updated throughout development; included in IB and Nonclinical Overview

S8.title: Immunotoxicity Studies for Human Pharmaceuticals
S8.scope: New chemical entities (small molecules) only; excludes biologics (S6), allergenicity, drug-specific autoimmunity

requirements.study_type: Tier 1 -- Standard Toxicity Study immunotox screening
requirements.duration: As per standard repeat-dose tox program

endpoints.hematology: Total and absolute differential leukocyte counts
endpoints.histology: Thymus, spleen, draining lymph node, at least one additional lymph node, bone marrow (if warranted), Peyer's patch (oral), BALT (inhalation), NALT (inhalation/nasal)

requirements.trigger: Weight-of-evidence review identifies cause for concern
requirements.timing: Before Phase III; earlier if target population is immunocompromised

endpoints: Absolute numbers and percentages of lymphocyte subsets (flow cytometry or IHC)
requirements.timing: Before Phase III; can be added to standard repeat-dose studies

requirements.trigger: Immunophenotyping shows NK changes, increased viral infections, or other concern
requirements.species: Rodent (ex vivo from spleen or blood)
endpoints: Cytotoxicity against labeled target cells at multiple E:T ratios

requirements.species: Mice and rats (monkey DTH poorly reproducible)

woe_trigger_factors: Drug disposition (retention in immune system cells)

S9.title: Nonclinical Evaluation for Anticancer Pharmaceuticals
S9.scope: Pharmaceuticals for serious/life-threatening malignancies (advanced cancer); small molecules and biopharmaceuticals
S9.key_principle: Reduced/modified nonclinical programs vs standard M3 requirements

species.small_molecules: Usually rodent + non-rodent; single rodent may suffice for genotoxic drugs targeting rapidly dividing cells

requirements.duration: Approximates clinical schedule (see schedule table); NOT required to match clinical treatment duration

start_dose.small_molecules: 1/10 STD10 (rodent) or 1/6 HNSTD (non-rodent if more sensitive)
start_dose.scaling: Body surface area (small molecules) or body weight/AUC (biologics)

requirements.duration: 3 months MAXIMUM for advanced cancer (no 6-month or 9-month required)

requirements.approach: Incorporated into general tox studies; stand-alone S7A/S7B NOT called for
requirements.endpoints: Detailed clinical observations + ECG in non-rodents generally sufficient

requirements.requirement: NOT required for clinical trials in advanced cancer; available at marketing application
requirements.species: Single relevant species may suffice; if positive in one, second species NOT warranted
requirements.exception: Not essential for marketing of genotoxic drugs targeting rapidly dividing cells or well-characterized teratogenic class
special_conditions: Positive dose-range-finding study (even non-GLP) in one species sufficient for marketing
When NHP is only relevant species: WoE assessment preferred over default NHP EFD

requirements.requirement: NOT warranted for clinical trials or marketing in advanced cancer
requirements.alternative: Reproductive organ evaluation in general tox studies sufficient

requirements.requirement: Generally NOT warranted for clinical trials or marketing in advanced cancer

requirements.requirement_clinical_trials: NOT essential for clinical trials in advanced cancer
requirements.requirement_marketing: Should be performed per S2 for marketing
requirements.special: Positive Ames: in vivo genotox NOT warranted. Negative Ames + positive in vitro chromosome: in vivo should be considered

requirements.requirement: NOT warranted for advanced cancer therapeutics
requirements.exception: Adjuvant setting with high cure rate: likely needed before marketing

nonclinical_treatment_schedule_table.nonclinical: Daily x 5-7 days, alternating weeks (2 cycles)

ADC_specific.whole_ADC: Tested in at least one species
ADC_specific.payload_linker: Nature of payload determines additional studies
ADC_specific.TK: Measure ADC level, payload level, estimate free antibody
ADC_specific.plasma_stability: In vitro data in human and tox species before FIH
ADC_specific.FIH_dosing: Minimum 2 doses; interval once every 3-4 weeks
ADC_specific.starting_dose: 1/10 STD10 (rodent) or 1/6 HNSTD (non-rodent) for ADC by BSA

S10.title: Photosafety Evaluation of Pharmaceuticals
S10.scope: New APIs, new excipients for dermal formulations; excludes peptides/proteins/mAbs/ADCs/oligonucleotides

decision_rules.MEC_below_1000: NOT photoreactive; no further testing needed
decision_rules.MEC_at_or_above_1000: Potentially photoreactive; proceed to further evaluation

decision_rules.negative: Very low probability of phototoxicity; can support no further testing
decision_rules.positive: Flag for follow-up only (does not confirm phototoxicity)

special_conditions: Negative ROS cannot negate positive in vitro phototoxicity test

requirements.study_type: 3T3 Neutral Red Uptake Phototoxicity Test (3T3 NRU-PT)
requirements.irradiation: Simulated sunlight; UVA 5-20 J/cm2; UVB attenuated

decision_rules.PIF_2_to_5_or_MPE_0.10_to_0.15: Questionable; generally no further evaluation for systemic drugs
decision_rules.PIF_gt_5_or_MPE_gt_0.15: Positive; proceed to in vivo or clinical assessment

special_conditions: Negative in vivo supersedes positive in vitro
Pigmented skin for APIs binding melanin
Retinal histopath for drugs absorbing above 400 nm

requirements.purpose: Low levels in light-exposed tissues or short half-life can support no further photosafety testing

requirements.requirement: Clinical assessment in Phase 3 for dermal products with MEC > 1000
special_conditions: Nonclinical photoallergy NOT recommended
NOT recommended for systemic drugs

S11.title: Nonclinical Safety Testing in Support of Development of Paediatric Pharmaceuticals
S11.scope: Small molecules and biotechnology-derived pharmaceuticals for paediatric use; excludes tissue-engineered, gene/cellular therapies, vaccines

requirements.study_type: Weight of Evidence (WoE) assessment for JAS need
factors_favoring_JAS: Effects on developing organ systems suspected
Pharmacological target involved in organ development

requirements.study_type: Dose Range-Finding (DRF) study in juvenile animals
requirements.species: Same species planned for definitive JAS (preferably rodent)
Single species generally sufficient (rat preferred); two species for paediatric-first/only development
core_endpoints: Growth (body weight + long bone length at necropsy)
Anatomic pathology (gross, organ weights, microscopic: bone/marrow, brain, heart, kidney, liver, lung, ovary, testis with spermatogenic evaluation, GI)
Toxicokinetics (near beginning and end of dosing)
requirements.dosing_period: Covers critical/active periods of growth/development for target organ of concern; NOT directly tied to clinical treatment duration
requirements.post_treatment: Generally recommended for reversibility/delayed onset; full reversibility not required
requirements.timing: Before paediatric clinical trial if WoE warrants

S12.title: Nonclinical Biodistribution Considerations for Gene Therapy Products
S12.scope: GT products mediating effect by expression of transferred genetic materials (purified nucleic acids, modified microorganisms, ex vivo modified cells, genome editing, oncolytic viruses); excludes prophylactic vaccines, chemically synthesized oligonucleotides

requirements.species: Biologically relevant species/model supporting transfer and expression
requirements.dose: Highest dose = expected max toxicology dose; must equal or exceed anticipated max clinical dose

assay_methods.primary: Quantitative nucleic acid amplification (qPCR, digital PCR) -- vector genome DNA/RNA per cell equivalent
assay_methods.expression_product: ELISA, IHC, Western blot, ISH, flow cytometry, in vivo/ex vivo imaging (risk-based)

requirements.time_points: Multiple: peak, steady-state, clearance; replication-competent vectors: also second peak from replication + clearance
requirements.timing: Must be completed BEFORE first-in-human clinical trial
requirements.GLP: Stand-alone non-GLP acceptable; if part of GLP tox study, in-life/sample collection must be GLP

requirements.requirement: BD of GT product in gonads must be evaluated for both sexes
requirements.follow_up_if_persistent: Additional studies to determine GT product levels in germ cells (oocytes, sperm) vs non-germline cells
requirements.purpose: Inform risk of inadvertent germline modification

triggers: Significant dose increase exceeding max nonclinical dose

leveraging_existing_data: Same GT product for different indication may suffice (considering dose, regimen, ROA, promoter)
Same vector with different transgene can support waiver (with justification)

S13.title: Non-Clinical Safety Evaluation of Oligonucleotide-Based Therapeutics (CONCEPT PAPER)
S13.status: Concept paper only; guideline not yet written; target Step 4: November 2027
S13.scope: ASOs, siRNAs, miRNA mimics, saRNAs, aptamers, decoys, tRNA, CpG-motif ONTs; excludes guide RNA, vaccines, coding mRNA
planned_study_areas: PK/distribution studies (ONT-specific tissue distribution, interspecies scaling)
General toxicity (species selection, dosing regimen, high dose setting, duration)
ont_specific_issues: On-target exaggerated pharmacology: surrogates for on-target effects
Off-target effects: in silico/in vitro for hybridization-dependent off-targets

timing_matrix.description: Summary of when each study type is required relative to clinical development phases

before_FIH.study: Safety pharmacology core battery (CNS, CV, respiratory)

before_FIH.notes: Duration per schedule table; single rodent may suffice for genotoxic drugs

before_phase_3.notes: Before WOCBP in Phase 3; pEFD can defer

before_marketing_NDA_MAA.study: Carcinogenicity studies (2-year rat and/or transgenic mouse)
before_marketing_NDA_MAA.notes: Unless WoE waiver per S1B(R1); post-approval for life-threatening diseases
Not needed for biotech (S6: 6 months max) or cancer (S9: 3 months max)
Not needed for clinical trials but required for marketing
Not needed for clinical trials; available at marketing unless genotoxic/well-characterized class

not_required_for_advanced_cancer_S9: Genotoxicity for clinical trials (required for marketing only)
not_required_for_biotech_S6R1: Standard genotoxicity battery (unless ADC with organic linker)

rodent_mouse: Second carcinogenicity study (2-year or rasH2-Tg 6-month) -- S1B
Genotoxicity in vivo (alternative to rat) -- S2(R1)
non_rodent_dog_or_NHP: Safety pharmacology CV core battery -- S7A
NHP_specific: ePPND (when NHP is only relevant species) -- S6(R1)/S5(R3)
Indirect fertility assessment (3+ month repeat-dose) -- S6(R1)
Species relevance for biotech products -- S6(R1)

not_appropriate.adult_rat_mouse_for_QT: S7B -- primary repolarization current is Ito, not IKr
